items.title: Siri AI at WWDC 2026
items.summary:
Given how badly burned anyone who took Apple's 2024 WWDC Apple Intelligence announcements at face value was, I'm holding to a strict "I'll believe it when I see it" policy for everything they announced today. 
The new Siri AI features do at least look feasible with today's technology, especially since Apple are licensing a custom Gemini-derived model that they can run on their own Private Cloud Compute.
It sounds like they'll be taking advantage of vision LLMs to extract information from the use

items.title: Say hi to "Siri AI"—Apple announces new, more "conversational" voice assistant
items.summary: New features coming this fall alongside two-tiered, Google-powered AI model overhaul.

items.title: Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity come to Google NotebookLM
items.summary: NotebookLM is getting a big upgrade, but it's only for AI Ultra and enterprise accounts right now.

items.title: For the 2nd time in weeks, Microsoft packages laced with credential stealer
items.summary: 73 packages run self-replicating stealer as soon as they're opened by an AI agent.

items.title: Confidential submission of draft S-1 to the SEC
items.summary: OpenAI confirms a confidential S-1 submission to the SEC and has not yet determined timing for further action.

items.title: "Chat is dead": OpenAI preps overhaul of ChatGPT
items.summary: OpenAI to recast hit chatbot as a route to higher-margin products before a potential IPO.

items.summary: Machine learning has its limits—how is it being used?

items.title: Built to benefit everyone: our plan
items.summary: A vision for the future of AI, focusing on access, safety, and shared prosperity as OpenAI works to ensure AGI benefits everyone.

items.title: Introducing the OpenAI Economic Research Exchange
items.summary: OpenAI launches the Economic Research Exchange to study AI’s impact on jobs, productivity, and the economy. Applications are now open for selected research projects.

items.title: The Open Source Community is backing OpenEnv for Agentic RL
datasette-agent-edit 0.1a0
items.summary:
Release: datasette-agent-edit 0.1a0
        I'm planning several plugins for Datasette Agent which can make edits to existing pieces of text - things like collaborative Markdown editing, updating large SQL queries, and editing SVG files.
Agentic editing of text is a little tricky to get right. My favorite published design for this is for the Claude text editor, which implements the following tools:

view - view sections of a file, with line numbers added to every line.
str_replace - find an exac
